2606.14585.title: Sensitivity Shaping for Latent Modeling
2606.14585.paper_summary: Generative dynamics models enable planning in challenging robotic systems, but safe deployment requires reliably detecting policy-induced out-of-distribution (OOD) transitions. Existing methods typically treat the learned dynamics as fixed and attach post hoc support surrogates. We show that these surrogates can fail when the dynamics are locally insensitive to critical action choices: unsupported control actions may produce latent predictions that resemble demonstrated transitions, suppressing OOD signals despite large true predictive errors. To address this, we introduce support-conditioned control-sensitivity regularization, which promotes sensitive local response to control input changes in learned dynamics in high-support training regions. This preserves control-induced variation while limiting unstable extrapolation due to weak empirical support. Experiments in vision-based obstacle avoidance, manipulation, and real-robot navigation show improved OOD detection and safer closed-loop planning.
2606.14585.paper_summary_zh: 生成動態模型使得在具有挑戰性的機器人系統中進行規劃成為可能，但安全部署需要可靠地檢測政策引起的分佈外（OOD）轉變。現有的方法通常將學習到的動態視為固定的，並附加事後支持代理。我們展示了當動態對關鍵行動選擇局部不敏感時，這些代理可能會失效：不支持的控制行動可能產生類似於已示範轉變的潛在預測，儘管真實的預測誤差很大，卻壓制了OOD信號。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了支持條件控制敏感性正則化，這促進了在高支持訓練區域中對控制輸入變化的敏感局部響應。這保留了控制引起的變異，同時限制了由於經驗支持不足而導致的不穩定外推。在基於視覺的障礙物避免、操作和真實機器人導航的實驗中，顯示了改進的OOD檢測和更安全的閉環規劃。
2606.14585.author: Hongzhan Yu et.al.
2606.14585.authors: Hongzhan Yu, Chenghao Li, Ruipeng Zhang, Henrik Christensen, Sicun Gao
